2026-04-17T10:07:23.8724996Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T10:07:23.8725449Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T10:07:23.8725824Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T10:07:23.8726136Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T10:07:23.8726369Z [36;1m[0m

The change is very large (42,313 additions / 32,726 deletions) and includes:\\n\\n- Regenerated all `Generated/` code from TypeSpec\\n- Added new custom model classes in `src/Custom/Models/`: `ArmDataBoxEdgeModelFactory`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger`\\n- Added a custom `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource.cs`\\n- Added new `CollectionResults/` classes (paginated collection helpers)\\n- Updated API surface (`api/*.cs` files) with 580 additions\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section is completely empty** — the migration and all API additions are unrecorded\\n- **README Examples section contains no code samples** — it only points to a generic link (`aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`); no service-specific example showing how to manage a `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`\\n- **README Key concepts section has no service-specific content** — it only links to generic SDK guidelines; it should describe the main resource types (`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `BandwidthScheduleResource`, etc.)\\n- **No sample test files exist** under `tests/Samples/` — needed for the snippet-injection tooling (`Update-Snippets.ps1`) to populate README examples\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit — fill in the `1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Migrated SDK code generation from AutoRest to TypeSpec.\\n- Added paginated collection result helpers for all list operations (e.g., `AddonsGetByRoleCollectionResult`, `AlertsGetByDataBoxEdgeDeviceCollectionResult`, `AvailableSkusGetAvailableSkusCollectionResult`, and others).\\n- Added custom model wrappers for read-only resources returned by device operations: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`.\\n- Added custom discriminated-union helpers for polymorphic types: `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`.\\n- Extended `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource` with additional device-level convenience operations.\\n- Updated `ArmDataBoxEdgeModelFactory` to expose factory methods for the new custom model types.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md`\\n\\nEdit — replace the placeholder **Key concepts** and **Examples** sections with service-specific content. The full updated sections should read:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe following are the main ARM resource types exposed by this library:\\n\\n| Resource class | Represents |\\n|---|---|\\n| `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource` | A Data Box Edge physical device registered in Azure |\\n| `BandwidthScheduleResource` | A bandwidth throttling schedule for a device |\\n| `DataBoxEdgeJobResource` | An asynchronous job running on a device (e.g., firmware update) |\\n| `DataBoxEdgeOrderResource` | The order placed for a device |\\n| `DataBoxEdgeAlertResource` | An alert raised by a device |\\n| `DataBoxEdgeStorageAccountResource` | A storage account endpoint configured on the device |\\n| `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource` | A compute role (IoT, Kubernetes, etc.) running on the device |\\n\\nEach resource type supports the standard Azure Resource Manager CRUD operations. Paginated list results are returned as `AsyncPageable` or `Pageable`.\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following example shows how to list all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription and print their names.\\n\\n```\\nArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nSubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\nawait foreach (DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device in subscription.GetDataBoxEdgeDevicesAsync())\\n{\\n    Console.WriteLine(device.Data.Name);\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe following example shows how to get a specific device and retrieve its current properties.\\n\\n```\\nArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = client.GetResourceGroupResource(\\n    ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;));\\n\\nDataBoxEdgeDeviceCollection devices = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices();\\nDataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await devices.GetAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Device: {device.Data.Name}, SKU: {device.Data.Sku?.Name}, Status: {device.Data.DataBoxEdgeDeviceStatus}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. Create a new sample test file at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` with the following content (use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as a canonical formatting reference):\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task ListDevices()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            await foreach (DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device in subscription.GetDataBoxEdgeDevicesAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine(device.Data.Name);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task GetDevice()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            string subscriptionId = TestEnvironment.SubscriptionId;\\n            string resourceGroupName = TestEnvironment.ResourceGroup;\\n            string deviceName = \\u0026#34;testDevice\\u0026#34;;\\n#endif\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = client.GetResourceGroupResource(\\n                ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName));\\n\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceCollection devices = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices();\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await devices.GetAsync(deviceName);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Device: {device.Data.Name}, SKU: {device.Data.Sku?.Name}, Status: {device.Data.DataBoxEdgeDeviceStatus}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n2. The README already has snippet placeholders added in Step 1 above (`Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices`, `Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice`). After writing the sample file, run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databoxedge` to inject the code.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` currently contains all required top-level sections but they need the following updates:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with service-specific resource table (see Step 1) |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ No code | Add two snippet placeholders (see Step 1) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databoxedge` — check the git diff to confirm the README snippet blocks were updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databoxedge` — no public API changes are expected from docs-only updates, but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24559337020/agentic_workflow) · ● 397.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58284\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T10:05:45Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T10:05:45Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-17T10:12:27.0479018Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T10:08:43.85182542Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":2,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4281573468,\"number\":58279,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[DataBoxEdge] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README after MPG migration to TypeSpec\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57614 by `@haiyuazhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57614 migrated `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec (MPG migration). The migration introduced:\\n\\n- **3 new resource types**: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource`\\n- **New custom models**: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger`\\n- **Significant API surface changes** to `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonResource`, `DataBoxEdgeOrderResource`, and other existing resource types\\n- **Modified enum type**: `DataBoxEdgeEncryptionAlgorithm` and `DataBoxEdgeSkuName` were reworked (43 and 116 deletions respectively)\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.2.0-beta.1` section is empty** — the section exists but has no entries under \\\"Features Added\\\", \\\"Breaking Changes\\\", \\\"Bugs Fixed\\\", or \\\"Other Changes\\\" despite substantial API additions and removals\\n- **README \\\"Key concepts\\\" section is generic** — links only to external Azure SDK docs with no DataBoxEdge-specific content describing available resource types (`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `BandwidthScheduleResource`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`, etc.)\\n- **README \\\"Examples\\\" section has no code samples** — only links to a generic `(aka.ms/redacted) URL; no DataBoxEdge-specific snippets for common operations\\n- **New resource types are undocumented** — `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`, and `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` are not mentioned in README or CHANGELOG\\n- **No `Samples/` directory** in the tests folder — no test-backed snippet files exist at all\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/CHANGELOG.md` — **edit**\\n\\nUpdate the `1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section to document the changes introduced by the TypeSpec migration:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource` exposing device capacity information.\\n- Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource` exposing device network settings.\\n- Added new resource type `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource` exposing device update summary.\\n- Added new model types: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger`.\\n- Migrated the library code generation from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n- `DataBoxEdgeEncryptionAlgorithm` enum values have been updated to align with the TypeSpec-generated API.\\n- `DataBoxEdgeSkuName` enum values have been updated to align with the TypeSpec-generated API.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the generic \\\"Key concepts\\\" and \\\"Examples\\\" sections with DataBoxEdge-specific content.\\n\\nReplace the existing \\\"Key concepts\\\" section body:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` library provides access to the following main resource types:\\n\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`**: Represents a Data Box Edge physical device. Supports CRUD operations as well as actions such as scanning for updates, triggering updates, and retrieving security settings.\\n- **`BandwidthScheduleResource`**: Manages bandwidth schedules on a device to control network usage during specified time windows.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeRoleResource`**: Manages compute roles (e.g., IoT, Kubernetes) running on the device.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonResource`**: Manages add-ons associated with a role (e.g., Arc for Kubernetes).\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeShareResource`**: Manages data shares (SMB or NFS) on the device.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeStorageAccountResource`**: Manages storage accounts configured on the device.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfoResource`**: Provides information about device resource capacity utilization.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettingsResource`**: Provides read access to the device's network configuration.\\n- **`DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummaryResource`**: Provides a summary of available updates for the device.\\n\\nFor general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts, see the [Azure Resource Manager documentation]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the existing \\\"Examples\\\" section body:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show code snippets for common scenarios.\\n\\n### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription\\n\\n```C# Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices\\n```\\n\\n### Get a specific Data Box Edge device\\n\\n```C# Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice\\n```\\n\\n### List bandwidth schedules for a device\\n\\n```C# Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules\\n```\\n\\nFor more samples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test file with sample code that the snippet tooling will inject into the README.\\n\\n#### New file: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/tests/Samples/Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_DataBoxEdgeDevice\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\\"Only verifying that the sample builds\\\")]\\n        public async Task ListDevices()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            await foreach (DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device in subscription.GetDataBoxEdgeDevicesAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\\"Device: {device.Data.Name}, Location: {device.Data.Location}\\\");\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\\"Only verifying that the sample builds\\\")]\\n        public async Task GetDevice()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\\"myResourceGroup\\\");\\n\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceCollection devices = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices();\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await devices.GetAsync(\\\"myDataBoxEdgeDevice\\\");\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\\"Device: {device.Data.Name}\\\");\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\\"  Model: {device.Data.ModelDescription}\\\");\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\\"  Status: {device.Data.DataBoxEdgeDeviceStatus}\\\");\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\\"Only verifying that the sample builds\\\")]\\n        public async Task ListBandwidthSchedules()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\\"myResourceGroup\\\");\\n\\n            DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices().GetAsync(\\\"myDataBoxEdgeDevice\\\");\\n\\n            await foreach (BandwidthScheduleResource schedule in device.GetBandwidthSchedules())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\\"Schedule: {schedule.Data.Name}\\\");\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README snippet placeholder locations:**\\n\\n- Section: `## Examples` → `### List all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription`\\n  - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListDevices`\\n- Section: `## Examples` → `### Get a specific Data Box Edge device`\\n  - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_GetDevice`\\n- Section: `## Examples` → `### List bandwidth schedules for a device`\\n  - Snippet name: `DataBoxEdge_ListBandwidthSchedules`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` currently has all required sections.
